entries.title: Brady Pettigrew evens the score against Cole Kelly and sends Bolingbrook to the sectional final
entries.summary: <p>At some point on Wednesday night or Thursday morning, Bolingbrook sophomore Brady Pettigrew is probably going to find a new inspirational photo to use as his phone screen.</p><p><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/high-school-basketball/2026/01/24/neuqua-valley-basketball-cole-kelly-mason-martin-bolingbrook" >Since Jan. 24</a> that valuable real estate in Pettigrew’s mind has been focused on Neuqua Valley generally and Cole Kelly specifically.</p><p>“He got me once,” Pettigrew said. “It has been on my phone screen since. I was waiting for this game. Credit to him, he had a great game. He didn’t hit as many shots as he would like, but he’s a great player. Everyone saw that.”</p><p>Pettigrew and Kelly are top 40 sophomores in the national class of 2028 and treated the sold-out crowd at the Class 4A Bolingbrook sectional to a show.</p><p>The game featured multiple lead changes in both halves, but the No. 8 Raiders and Pettigrew dominated the fourth quarter to beat No. 5 Neuqua Valley 68-53 on <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/high-school-basketball/scores/2026/03/04/ihsa-state-basketball-playoff-scores-illinois" >Wednesday</a>.</p><p>Pettigrew finished with 25 points on 7 of 11 shooting. Kelly had 17 points and seven rebounds and shot 5-for-19.</p><p>“When you are an elite player, you want to show that you are an elite player and [Pettigrew] did that tonight,” Bolingbrook coach Rob Brost said. “He is getting more mature with his game. The efficiency was his next thing and he is doing that.”</p><p>Bolingbrook (25-7) led by nine midway through the third quarter. Kelly scored seven points in a 13-1 run that put Neuqua Valley ahead 46-43. Pettigrew tied the game with three free throws to close out the third quarter.</p><p>“We didn’t defend [Pettigrew] very well,” Neuqua Valley coach Todd Sutton said. “He had open shots early. Our defense was just off. Give them credit. That is not normal for us.”</p><p>The fourth quarter was all Bolingbrook. The Raiders are taller, faster and deeper than Neuqua Valley and it showed. Pettigrew threw down two impressive dunks in the final quarter, and Jeremy McCullum had one.</p><p>“It was a great team win,” Pettigrew said. “Our coaches came through with some great play calls down the stretch. I love them to death, and I feel that they have my back.”</p><p>Jayden Madden, a 6-9 senior, had nine points and 13 rebounds. He led the charge on the Raiders’ 34-20 advantage on the boards.</p><p>“[Madden] has really been coming on of late,” Brost said. “It has been a different season for him personally. He was hurt and then he was sick and then he got sick a second time. He’s finally healthy and putting it all together. He makes a big difference.”</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-640000" class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Neuqua Valley’s Mason Martin hits a long shot against Bolingbrook.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Allen Cunningham/For the Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>Bolingbrook played a challenging schedule against several high-level national teams and then spent a portion of the season without point guard Trey Brost, so they have been just outside the handful of elite teams at the top of the Super 25. With Brost and Madden both back and clicking, the Raiders are peaking at the right time.</p><p>“Today I just brought it all,” Madden said. “I didn’t want to go home. [The season] has been hard, but I had the mentality to push through and keep going. The team relies on me, so I have to show up. I know my length and my style of play, so I went out there and used it to my advantage.”</p><p>Illinois recruit Mason Martin had 13 points and four rebounds for Neuqua Valley (32-2) and junior Danny Mikuta scored 11 points.</p><p>“You can’t stop Mason and Cole for a whole game, but we wanted to limit them,” Pettigrew said. “Give credit to our fans. It was packed; they all came out. Credit to the Bolingbrook community.”</p><p>Bolingbrook will host top-ranked Benet in the sectional final on Friday. The Raiders student section chanted “we want Benet” as the final seconds of the game ticked away.</p><p>“Everybody wants Benet,” Pettigrew said. “That’s the team to beat.”</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" > <div class="TweetUrl"> <blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">All jammed up at Bolingbrook and ready to go. <a href="https://t.co/0syyl3R1ot">pic.twitter.com/0syyl3R1ot</a></p>— Michael O'Brien (@michaelsobrien) <a href="https://twitter.com/michaelsobrien/status/2029360508088840310?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">March 5, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script> </div> </div> </div></div><p></p>
entries.author: Michael O'Brien

entries.title: Blackhawks acquire 2027 first-round pick from Oilers for Jason Dickinson, Colton Dach
entries.summary: <p>The <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/blackhawks" target="_blank" >Blackhawks</a> ended up doing the things during NHL trade-deadline week they were expected to do. They just didn't wait until the deadline Friday afternoon to do them.</p><p>Forward Jason Dickinson was reportedly traded Wednesday night to the Oilers — a move the veteran center had spent the last month <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/blackhawks/2026/03/03/blackhawks-jason-dickinson-nhl-trade-deadline-rumors-kings" target="_blank" >mentally preparing</a> for.</p><p>In Edmonton, he will join former Hawks teammate Connor Murphy, who was dealt there Monday after also spending the last month mentally preparing for it.</p><p>In the Dickinson deal, Hawks general manager Kyle Davidson managed to convince ex-Hawks-turned-Oilers GM Stan Bowman to give up a 2027 first-round pick — an impressive return. (He received a 2028 second-round pick for Murphy.)</p><p>Getting that impressive return, however, required four concessions around the edges.</p><p>For one, young forward Colton Dach was sent to the Oilers alongside Dickinson. That might actually be the most surprising part of the Hawks' deadline week, but it's hardly shocking nonetheless.</p><p>The Hawks also took on overpaid forward Andrew Mangiapane and retained 50% of Dickinson's $4.25 million salary-cap hit, using up their last remaining retention slot for this season.</p><p>And the first-round pick acquired by the Hawks has top-12 protection, meaning it could slide to 2028 if the Oilers miss the playoffs in 2027.</p><p>Mangiapane, who has declined tremendously since his 35-goal outburst in 2021-22, signed a two-year contract carrying a $3.6 million cap hit with the Oilers last summer, and it has aged poorly. </p><p>The 29-year-old had tallied just 14 points (and poor analytics) in 52 NHL games this season before clearing waivers and going down to the AHL on Sunday.</p><p>The Hawks could keep Mangiapane as a reclamation project or AHL scorer. But they could buy him out this summer instead, which would incur a $2.93 million cap hit next season and a $333,000 cap hit the following season. That wouldn't be an issue with their abundance of cap space.</p><p>Dickinson's Hawks tenure ends after four seasons, during which he brought stability to the team's third-line center role and handled their toughest defensive matchups.</p><p>The pending free agent finishes with 44 goals and 50 assists in 266 games as a Hawk, with half of those goals coming in his unrepeated 2023-24 offensive breakout season.</p><p>Wins were scarce during his Hawks tenure, but he seemed to thoroughly enjoy it. His intelligent, analytical mind made him a valuable resource in the locker room, too.</p><p><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/blackhawks/2026/03/03/blackhawks-jason-dickinson-nhl-trade-deadline-rumors-kings" target="_blank" >He said Wednesday</a> he would enjoy an opportunity to return to the playoffs, which the Oilers likely will provide.</p><p>"I think my game is well-built for playoffs," Dickinson said. "It’s simple. It’s direct. Teams can want something like that to bolster depth and give them strength on the [penalty kill] instead of big-fish hunting for a dynamic player."</p><p>Dach, an Edmonton native originally drafted by Bowman, had waffled in and out of the Hawks' lineup since January and exited the Olympic break expressing a desire to <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/blackhawks/2026/02/22/blackhawks-colton-dach-ice-time-nhl-free-agency-landon-slaggert" target="_blank" >earn more playing time</a>.</p><p>He tallied nine points and a team-leading 189 hits in 53 games. That physicality will be missed, but Landon Slaggert — who can kill penalties — had already leapfrogged him in the hierarchy, and a number of incoming prospects would've likely done the same soon.</p><p>With Mangiapane coming back, that last note is important. The Hawks still needed to accomplish their overarching deadline objective of freeing up roster spots for soon-to-arrive forwards Anton Frondell and Sacha Boisvert (not to mention Nick Lardis).</p><p>And eventually, forward prospect AJ Spellacy, who will turn pro next season, should be able to provide the same physicality Dach did.</p><p>With two days left until the deadline, the only remaining question is whether Davidson will do anything more. Trading forward Ilya Mikheyev remains a possibility, but the lack of a salary-retention slot would complicate it.</p><p></p><p></p><p></p><p></p><bsp-list-loadmore class="PageListH" data-module entries.author: Ben Pope

entries.title: Lou Holtz, 89, led Notre Dame back to the top and left college football fans laughing — most of the time
entries.summary: <p>How seriously did <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/college-sports/2026/03/04/former-notre-dame-football-coach-lou-holtz-dies-at-age-89" target="_blank" >coach Lou Holtz</a> have <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/college-sports" target="_blank" >Notre Dame football</a> rolling back in the day?</p><p>In 1992, just to pick a year out of a hat, he signed the No. 1-ranked freshman class in the nation for the fifth time in six years.</p><p>“As faxes arrived from Mississippi, Texas, Florida and elsewhere, Holtz counted 18 All-Americas in a class of 22 freshman prospects,” the Sun-Times reported. “Miami [was next with] nine, and Florida State and Florida eight each.”</p><p>It didn’t stop Holtz, who coached the Fighting Irish for 11 seasons, from bemoaning his own class’ renown, saying at the time, “People who get excited about recruiting put these kids in the Hall of Fame.”</p><p>Holtz built the Irish back into a superpower — winning the 1988 national championship, still their most recent one — while becoming an “icon,” as friend Joe Theismann called him, as much for his sense of humor and storytelling as for his 100 wins at the school from 1986 through 1996.</p><p>The College Football Hall of Famer died Wednesday at 89 in Orlando.</p><p>“Lou was a true icon in college football and an incredible public speaker,” Theismann, a Notre Dame all-time great quarterback, told the Sun-Times. “So many of the things that Lou would share with his team and share with people will go down in history as some of the best, funniest stories ever told. He was so quick-witted, so smart, that when he started talking, you just became captivated.</p><p>“He was a true personality. Although he was small of stature, he was larger than life.”</p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/college-sports/2026/03/04/former-notre-dame-football-coach-lou-holtz-dies-at-age-89" target="_blank" >Former Notre Dame football coach Lou Holtz dies at age 89</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p>Holtz led four schools to Top 25 finishes and an NCAA-record six schools to bowl games. His seven stops as a head coach included NC State, Minnesota, South Carolina and one ill-fated season in the NFL with the 1976 Jets, who went 3-10. At Notre Dame, Holtz had winning streaks of 23, 21, 20 and 17 games. In all, he won 249 college games.</p><p>He was born in 1937 in Follansbee, West Virginia, and, along the way, acquired an unprecedented ability to paint his heavily favored teams’ outlooks as hopeless. When quarterback Rick Mirer — the decorated successor to championship-winning QB Tony Rice — was considering an early jump to the NFL after the 1991 season, Holtz said doing so would be a “catastrophe” for the Irish.</p><p>“A wide receiver is a hubcap,” he said. “A quarterback is the engine.”</p><p>Mirer ended up staying and became the No. 2 overall pick in 1993, later playing for the Bears in 1997.</p><p>“Lou Holtz was more than our coach. He was a fearless leader and encouraged us in life,” Mirer shared in a text message. “His message never changed: trust, love, commitment. He will be missed.”</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" > <div class="TweetUrl"> <blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">Remembering the life and legacy of Lou Holtz<br><br>The man who always reminded us to play like a champion 💛☘️<br><br>We love you, Coach.<a href="https://twitter.com/hashtag/GoIrish?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">#GoIrish</a>☘️ <a href="https://t.co/iZi7DUsiG4">pic.twitter.com/iZi7DUsiG4</a></p>— Notre Dame Football (@NDFootball) <a href="https://twitter.com/NDFootball/status/2029315939263504763?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">March 4, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script> </div> </div> </div></div><p>After a 1992 upset loss to Stanford, Holtz ripped himself as having gone “possibly brain dead.” He swore he’d play his entire second-string offense in the first half must-win game the next week against Pitt, and he did in a 52-21 win.</p><p>Northwestern great Pat Fitzgerald, now coach at Michigan State, expressed “great respect” for Holtz, whose staff recruited the Sandburg linebacker in the early 1990s. On the way to the Rose Bowl in 1995, Northwestern, led by Fitzgerald, pulled off an astonishing, season-opening 17-15 upset in South Bend, prompting Holtz to say, “Other than the assassination, how was the play, Mrs. Lincoln?”</p><p>Holtz followed the demoralizing Gerry Faust era with some of Notre Dame’s brightest seasons. The famed “Play Like a Champion” sign at Notre Dame stadium was hung on his watch. But when he announced his resignation at 59 in November 1996, he was evasive, some believed, as to why.</p><p>“I thought this would be the end of my life,” he said then. “My mother always felt when you coached at Notre Dame, you coached until you died. And then you went to heaven.”</p><p>After two years working for CBS Sports, Holtz returned to coaching at South Carolina. He later returned to television at ESPN.</p><p>In 2020, Holtz received the nation’s highest civilian honor, the Presidential Medal of Freedom, from outgoing President Donald Trump. Holtz was a staunch supporter and had spoken at the Republican National Convention, where he slammed Democrat Joe Biden as a Catholic “in name only” and called Trump the “greatest president of my lifetime.”</p><p>Holtz’s family released a statement saying he is “remembered for his enduring values of faith, family, service and an unwavering belief in the potential of others.”</p>
entries.author: Steve Greenberg

entries.title: Chicagoans uneasy about gas prices as Iran war disrupts supply chain
entries.summary: <p>It took less than a week for gas prices to spike after the U.S and Israel launched a major attack on Iran on Saturday.</p><p>As the war continues, Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz, the narrow mouth of the Persian Gulf where about a fifth of the world's oil passes through. Any disruption to traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is highly disruptive to oil shipments. </p><p>On Wednesday, the average price in Chicago for a regular gallon of gas was $3.51, or 17 cents higher than the week prior, according to AAA. The national average was $3.20, up by 20 cents<b> </b>compared to the week prior.</p><p>“I continue to watch the Strait of Hormuz. That's really what is driving the current situation," said Patrick De Haan, head of petroleum analysis at GasBuddy. "It’s a vital waterway, which carries about 20% to 25% of the total amount of oil consumed on a global basis every day. … As goes the situation in the Strait, so go oil and gas prices.”</p><p>De Haan predicts average gas prices may go up further by 10 cents or 20 cents. He said another increase will occur due to the required annual transition to "summer" gas.</p><p>"I think a lot of people are thinking that we're going to see some massive spike back to $4 or $5 or $6," he said. “Oil prices today are actually down a little bit, but we also have the seasonality that's starting to impact prices too.”</p><p>Chicago's average gas price of $3.51 is still about 12 cents lower compared to this time last year. Still, the recent double-digit spike is concerning for a number of Chicago area residents, including some that remembered the record-high prices in 2022. </p><p>Portage Park resident Nahva Bropleh, 34, is a real estate leasing agent. He estimates he drives his 2021 Kia Soul about 20 miles per day for work. His wife also has car. </p><p>“I'm definitely afraid of prices getting higher. I mean, I have to drive for work. So I can't really avoid not getting gas. Our other car is a four-wheel drive that takes up more gas than this,” said Bropleh, as he pumped about $10 of gas at a Shell station in Lake View, where the price for regular gasoline was $3.49.</p><p>If gas prices climb, he anticipates cutting back on date nights and recreational travel.</p><p>“It's just tough, especially when you got family that's not in state and you want to try to visit them,” he said.</p><p>Illinois has the eighth-highest average gas price in the country at $3.32, according to AAA. California ($4.74) and Hawaii ($4.42) took the crown for the most expensive at No. 1 and No. 2, respectively.</p><p>Illinois often has higher gas prices than the national average because it has the second-highest taxes on gas in the country, according to Illinois Policy.</p><p>“These increases can add up to spending $30 more a month on gas,” Gay Held, 70, said. “I pray it doesn’t get out of control.”</p><p>Held doesn’t drive as much anymore, but she'll often fill up even if her car's tank is half full.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/0822318/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6826x3831+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F29%2Fef%2F17c09729419792a0a4ca751783b9%2Fgas-260305-01.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="Gay Held, 70, of Lake View, makes a stop at a Lake View Shell station." srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/0822318/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6826x3831+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F29%2Fef%2F17c09729419792a0a4ca751783b9%2Fgas-260305-01.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/8a3d7f9/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6826x3831+0+0/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F29%2Fef%2F17c09729419792a0a4ca751783b9%2Fgas-260305-01.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/0822318/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6826x3831+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F29%2Fef%2F17c09729419792a0a4ca751783b9%2Fgas-260305-01.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Gay Held, 70, of Lake View, makes a stop at a Lake View Shell station.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Ashlee Rezin/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>“It'll be a little less expensive [because] I know the next time I fill it's gonna be higher, possibly. We'll see what happens with Hormuz,” Held said.</p><p>President Donald Trump pitched a plan Tuesday aimed at getting oil and trade moving again through the Strait.</p><p>He said on social media that he ordered the U.S. International Development Finance Corp. to provide political risk insurance for tankers carrying oil and other goods through the Persian Gulf “at a very reasonable price.” Political risk insurance is a type of coverage intended to protect firms against financial losses caused by unstable political conditions, government actions, or violence. Marine insurers had been canceling or raising rates for insurance in the region.</p><p>He said, if necessary, the U.S. Navy would escort oil tankers through the Strait. </p><h3>Flashback to 2022</h3><p>Irwin Toledo, 22, fears a spike higher than in 2022, when Chicago saw record-high unleaded gas prices of up to $6.07, largely due to the war in Ukraine. </p><p>“I think it’s going to get a lot worse,” he said. “I could always walk to work instead, I guess.”</p><p>At a nearby pump, Ed O’Brien said he relies on his fuel rewards card for savings.</p><p>O’Brien, 54, drives his 2021 Kia a lot for his job in sales, estimating a weekly mileage of 150. He spends about $30 for a full tank. While he used his rewards card, which he signed up for in 2022, he still spent about $30 for a full tank on Wednesday.</p><p>Brett Bakasov, a chauffeur with a 2024 Chevrolet Suburban, pays for gas out-of-pocket. His job is his main source of income. He said he racks up about 200 miles daily and spends about $90 for a full tank, which he fills up every couple days.</p><p>“I normally fill up at Costco,” the Schaumburg resident said. </p><p>Costco is known for having cheaper gas — the Lincoln Park location advertised regular gas at $3.25.</p><p>“I’m concerned, but, I mean, I cannot change the gas prices,” he said. “I can work more hours to make more, though. Driving is a necessity; it pays my bills.”</p><p>Jada Brown, assistant manager at the Lake View Shell station, hasn't noticed much of a price increase yet. She remembers how angry customers were in 2022 when prices jumped.</p><p>"People act like it's my fault," she said. "They say I need to talk to somebody, and I shouldn't raise them prices. I say, I can't go back there and change prices."</p><p>While the future of gas prices is uncertain and dependent on a number of events, particularly the war in Iran, there are things drivers can do to conserve gas, according to Molly Hart, spokesperson for the Illinois chapter of AAA.</p><p>“You could combine your errands and do it all in one swoop to save on gas. If you have anything heavy in the car, that's going to put more pressure on the gas,” she said. “And speeding, aggressive driving depletes your gas in the tank.”</p><p>Hart also recommended downloading mobile apps that map nearby gas stations and prices to help find the lowest price.</p><p>“It’s a watch and see. It’s really too early to predict what it’s going to look like,” she said. “We aren’t anticipating [higher prices] but … I don’t want to give false hope.”</p><p><i>Contributing: AP</i></p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/news/2026/02/28/protesters-in-loop-condemn-u-s-attack-on-iran-war-is-not-the-solution" >Protesters in Loop condemn U.S. attack on Iran: ‘War is not the solution’</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p></p>
entries.author: Mariah Rush

entries.title: Cubs No. 5 prospect Jefferson Rojas' swing turning heads in major-league camp — 'Wow'
entries.summary: <p>PHOENIX — About 20 minutes before <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/cubs" >Cubs</a> prospect Jefferson Rojas was set to face right-hander Cade Horton in live batting practice early this spring, the <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/cubs/2025/11/10/cubs-cade-horton-finishes-second-in-nl-rookie-of-the-year-voting-is-poised-to-play-pivotal-role-in-2026" >NL Rookie of the Year runner-up</a> had a request: “Hey, go easy on me.”</p><p>Rojas didn't exactly oblige. </p><p>On a backfield at the Cubs’ Mesa complex, with a gaggle of fans, coaches and players looking on, Rojas launched a home run off Horton.</p><p>“I told you!” one of Rojas’ minor-league teammates said to another as they passed.</p><p>Horton grinned at the mention of the encounter weeks later. He estimated that he’s faced Rojas seven times over the years. Rojas homered in three of those match-ups, all on different pitches.</p><p>“Jefferson, he owns me,” Horton said. “Like, all right, dude, we get it! You're a good hitter.</p><p>“But he's really skilled for his age, and just really mature for his age. So I'm happy to have him on our team.”</p><p>Rojas, ranked the Cubs’ No. 5 prospect by MLB Pipeline, is the youngest Cubs player in major-league camp this year. The 20-year-old non-roster invitee is already turning heads.</p><p>“Jefferson's made a great impression,” manager Craig Counsell said. “Just being able to see Jefferson two years ago, and where we're at right now — wow. … We've got a good offensive player here that's going to impact the baseball and do some damage. He's going to be a dangerous hitter.”</p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/cubs/2024/03/15/cubs-prospect-jefferson-rojas-rise-youngest-starter-canceled-cubs-white-sox-spring-breakout" >Cubs infield prospect Jefferson Rojas on the rise</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p>In addition to that backfield live batting practice home run, Rojas homered in two Cactus League games. Both have been no-doubters.</p><p>The most recent helped lift the Cubs over the Brewers on Wednesday at American Family Field of Phoenix. He crushed a changeup 438 feet, according to Statcast.</p><p>Rojas agreed that the big-leagues and Wrigley Field don't feel all that distant anymore.</p><p>“Getting to practice with players that play out there, it definitely feels closer,” he said through team interpreter Fredy Quevedo Jr.</p><p>Cubs hitting coach Dustin Kelly, formerly a minor-league coordinator, watched Rojas play in the Dominican Republic when he was about 17 years old.</p><p>“You could tell that he was going to be a good player, but he was so raw,” Kelly told the Sun-Times. “And just seeing him out here, how hard he's hitting the ball, where his swing has come — his swing looks so good right now. So it's really, really fun.”</p><p>Rojas’ listed height and weight, 5-foot-11 and 150 pounds, is certainly outdated. But he’s not exactly physically imposing. His power comes from a combination of weight lifting and medicine-ball training, and efficient swing mechanics.</p><p>“A lot of the work that he's done down in the minor leagues is getting into his legs and using his lower half and creating a little bit of posture,” Kelly said. “So, his swing path is really cleaned up, and just the overall efficiency of his swing, where it is right now, is generating some output. He's hitting the ball really hard — much harder than what I thought or expected from him, especially in a major-league camp at 20 years old.”</p><p>The day before, Kelly had double-checked Rojas’ age, not quite believing that he still was only 20.</p><p>Rojas hasn’t yet played above Double-A, getting 39 games at that level under his belt last year. Naturally, his production dipped as he adjusted. He posted a .485 OPS after owning a .871 OPS in Single-A to start the season.</p><p>“You’ve got a lot more more more prospects in there,” he said, “so it's definitely a lot more competitive than what you’ve got in South Bend.”</p><p>So, the big-leagues aren’t calling quite yet. But Rojas has definitely capture the major-league staff’s attention.</p><p></p><bsp-listcarousel class="PageListE" data-module data-column-count=3 data-hide-authors="true" data-hide-categories="true" entries.title: Horoscope for Thursday, March 05, 2026
entries.summary: <h2>Moon Alert</h2> <p>Avoid shopping (except food and gas) or major decisions after 5 p.m. The moon is in Libra.</p><h2>Aries (March 21-April 19)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Today it's easy for you to have a positive outlook and feel more optimistic than usual. Relations with spouses, partners and close friends will be friendly. Be aware of the unusually long moon alert, which begins today and lasts for most of tomorrow. (Yikes.)</p><h2>Taurus (April 20-May 20)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Enjoy socializing with friends, groups and organizations, whether for personal reasons or work-related associations, because you will have a good time talking to others. Conferences, conventions and rallies will appeal. Interface with others, but wait until Saturday to act.</p><h2>Gemini (May 21-June 20)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> You can make a great impression on bosses, parents, teachers and VIPs today — including the police — because you look fortunate, informed, capable and affluent. You might not feel this way, but this is what you project. It's a fabulous day to socialize. Enjoy your good fortune!</p><h2>Cancer (June 21-July 22)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Do something to expand your world today. Travel, learn, talk to different people, see different places — kick things up a notch because you want adventure and a chance to broaden your horizons. Group activities in particular will appeal. Find your people — join the tribe.</p><h2>Leo (July 23-Aug. 22)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Financial discussions will go well today. In fact, this is a good day to negotiate details about shared property, inheritances, or anything you own jointly with someone else. This includes debt. Listen to suggestions from someone, and don’t hesitate to make some yourself.</p><h2>Virgo (Aug. 23-Sept. 22)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> With the help of friends or your involvement with a group, you might see ways to boost your income now or down the road. Possibly a class, seminar or conference will inspire you. Talk to spouses, partners and close friends to share your hunches and opinions.</p><h2>Libra (Sept. 23-Oct. 22)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Today the moon is in your sign, and the planets are favorably aligned, which gives you a pleasant sense of well-being. You'll find it easy to be warm and friendly to others. Be aware that today what you give is what you will get back magnified. (It's important to know this.)</p><h2>Scorpio (Oct. 23-Nov. 21)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Accept all invitations to party and socialize with others because this is a fun-loving day full of opportunities to enjoy groups and organizations. Playful times with kids, the arts, sports, and opportunities for vacations or fun excursions are all excellent choices. Travel if possible.</p><h2>Sagittarius (Nov. 22-Dec. 21)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Invite others to your home today because it's a great day for meetings or group projects. Others might help you make home improvements or redecorate. No matter what happens, good feelings and harmony with others will create a sense of pulling together to get things done.</p><h2>Capricorn (Dec. 22-Jan. 19)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Writers, actors, teachers and those of you in sales and marketing will excel today because your optimism and enthusiasm will carry the day. People will be attracted to you and want to hear what you have to say. This is also a great day to learn something new. Travel will appeal.</p><h2>Aquarius (Jan. 20-Feb. 18)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Look for ways to boost your income today. Be open to the advice of others because friends, even a group, might have something meaningful to say. Work-related travel is likely. You might also enjoy spending money. It's your call.</p><h2>Pisces (Feb. 19-March 20)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> This is a wonderful day because the sun is in your sign dancing with lucky Jupiter, which, in turn, is dancing with the moon. This means everything is coming together to promote good feelings of trust and conviviality. People are happy to share good times!</p><h2>If today is your birthday</h2><p>Actor, comedian and author Aasif Mandvi (1966) shares your birthday today. You are charming, sympathetic, and caring. You need to feel happy and secure. This is the final year of a nine-year cycle for you. Take inventory of your life. Let go of people, places, and things from the past that have held you back. Do some internal and external housecleaning. </p>
entries.author: Georgia Nicols

entries.title: Man arrested in West Humboldt Park shooting
entries.summary: <p>A man was arrested after he allegedly shot another man during an argument in West Humboldt Park Wednesday night, according to Chicago police.</p><p>The man who was arrested, 41, was in an argument with a 60-year-old man in the 4100 block of West Potomac Avenue at 7:53 p.m. when the younger man shot the elder man in the leg, police said. The man who was shot was taken to Stroger Hospital in good condition.</p><p>Officers later arrested the 41-year-old. Charges are pending, police said.</p>
entries.author: Sun-Times Wire

entries.title: After pushing the WNBA forward, Unrivaled faces its next test
entries.summary: <p>Unrivaled arrived in early 2025 as a novel women’s basketball delight. For the first time, fans could watch their favorite players play stateside in the winter, in a full-court 3-on-3 format most hadn’t seen before.</p><p>“It’s different, right?” Geno Auriemma, UConn coach and Unrivaled investor, told the Sun-Times.</p><p>Unrivaled drew a strong contrast to the WNBA, not just in format but in player experience. That difference became a key bargaining chip in collective-bargaining negotiations.</p><p>As WNBA players pushed for a new economic model that rewarded them for the sport’s rising popularity, they could point to the startup league.</p><p>Look what we’ve got going on over here in Miami! State-of-the-art facilities. High salaries. Players made equity partners from the start instead of being told to wait until the league hit a certain scale. </p><p>A league by players, for players, as co-founder Napheesa Collier called it.</p><p>Though CBA negotiations have been <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/wnba/2025/12/26/three-forces-shaping-the-wnbas-stalled-cba-negotiations" >tense and prolonged</a>, real gains have emerged. For the first time, WNBA players will share in league revenue without needing to hit specific targets. Max salaries will reach at least $1 million. Minimum standards for facilities are coming.</p><p>The question now is what Unrivaled will look like in an ecosystem it helped reshape.</p><h3>TV vs. touring</h3><p>Like true startup founders, Collier and her co-founder Breanna Stewart talk about the future as a work in progress. They are still figuring out the right number of teams and the right mix of tour stops.</p><p>Its current form raises tough questions. None of the league's eight teams represent a specific place, which limits fan attachment.</p><p>The 3-on-3 format also has its limits. Why watch what league President Alex Bazzell calls “enhanced pick-up” when a blue-chip college matchup is also on TV?</p><p>For many fans, Unrivaled didn’t offer a clear enough answer.</p><p>TV ratings declined in Year 2, with the league <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/chicago-sky/2026/02/11/angel-reese-rejoins-3x3-league-unrivaled-with-mid-season-deal" >averaging about 149,000 viewers as of Feb. 11.</a> The league said its TV partner, TNT, largely expected that dip. But growth will need to resume to keep the partnership strong.</p><p>The good news is that Unrivaled’s second season validated demand for the live product. And it hinted at why the future of women's basketball may borrow from the past.</p><p>Before women’s professional leagues existed, teams toured from city to city. Unrivaled returned to these barnstorming roots with plenty of success. Tour stops in New York and Philadelphia both sold out, with the Philadelphia event setting an attendance record for a women’s basketball game.</p><p>Place was a key part of the appeal. For a night, fans and players could celebrate their connection to a city.</p><p>Philadelphia, which does not yet have a WNBA team, became a homecoming for Rose BC star Kahleah Copper. Copper grew up playing on Philadelphia courts and finally got to perform in front of family and friends.</p><p>“The guys I used to hoop with, they watch me on TV,” Copper said on the “Bird’s Eye View” podcast. “Half of them have never been out the city, haven't gone anywhere. So for them to see me in person, from this little girl who really couldn't hoop, who they really shaped — that is so dope.”</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-900000" name="image-900000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/3070f5c/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3490x1959+0+0/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F4c%2Ff2%2Fecc9241f4e0d922114967698fe01%2Fkah-unrivaled.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/74f884d/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3490x1959+0+0/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F4c%2Ff2%2Fecc9241f4e0d922114967698fe01%2Fkah-unrivaled.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/3f9c15b/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3490x1959+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F4c%2Ff2%2Fecc9241f4e0d922114967698fe01%2Fkah-unrivaled.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="Laces v Rose - Unrivaled 2026" srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/3f9c15b/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3490x1959+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F4c%2Ff2%2Fecc9241f4e0d922114967698fe01%2Fkah-unrivaled.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/1d3678f/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3490x1959+0+0/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F4c%2Ff2%2Fecc9241f4e0d922114967698fe01%2Fkah-unrivaled.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/3f9c15b/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3490x1959+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F4c%2Ff2%2Fecc9241f4e0d922114967698fe01%2Fkah-unrivaled.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>MEDLEY, FLORIDA - FEBRUARY 22: Kahleah Copper #2 of the Rose drives to the basket against Jackie Young #0 of the Laces during the first quarter of the Unrivaled 2026 game at Sephora Arena on February 22, 2026 in Medley, Florida. (Photo by Leonardo Fernandez/Getty Images) ORG XMIT: 776453536</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Leonardo Fernandez/Getty</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>While Philadelphia had been in the works for nearly a year, Brooklyn was essentially a pop-up. Even on three weeks' notice, celebrities filled the courtside seats. Upper-level fans invented a dance inspired by Liberty mascot Ellie the Elephant. Two of the league’s best shooters drilled game-winning threes.</p><p>Still, Unrivaled is not trying to become a fully touring league. Playing in one place is part of their pitch: a chance for players to slow down and reduce strain on their bodies.</p><p>But success has a way of changing minds.</p><p>“Let’s be frank: when we returned from Philly, there were four teams that were kind of jealous that the other four teams had the experience, and there were four teams who once we got back were like, 'When can we go again?'” Bazzell said before the semifinals. “So it’s funny, like one experience can change the mindset of an entire building.”</p><p>Unrivaled now plans to hold six to eight tour stops next year in both non-WNBA markets and existing ones.</p><h3>Does the future need all the stars?</h3><p>Unrivaled launched as a premium league featuring many of the sport’s top players. As Year 2 winds down, Bazzell still insists the league wants to be the home for the best talent.</p><p>But the landscape is changing. With top WNBA salaries set to exceed $1 million under the next CBA, will top players still want to suit up in the winter?</p><p>Rest has rarely been part of the professional women’s basketball cycle, but the sport’s biggest names may soon discover its value. (Some already have: Caitlin Clark and A’ja Wilson have never played in Unrivaled.)</p><p>Still, Unrivaled doesn’t need to panic quite yet.</p><p>On the men’s side, there is precedent for offseason leagues surviving without the very biggest stars.</p><p>NBA Summer League offers an interesting model. Each July, rookies, second-year players and G League prospects head to Las Vegas to compete. It’s become a basketball tourism destination and a modest TV draw. This past summer it averaged about 255,000 viewers, not much higher than Unrivaled’s inaugural season.</p><p>Climbing back to that level seems realistic.</p><p>Especially if Unrivaled leans into what worked best this season: the next generation.</p><p>In Year 2, Dominique Malonga, the 6-6 phenom, began to dunk confidently. Paige Bueckers, whom Auriemma has long urged to be more selfish, got comfortable taking over games.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-f90000" name="image-f90000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/b0d5115/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4973x2791+0+0/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F6f%2F4d%2Ff9adf3e14483ae37b446301a8510%2Fpaige-yelling.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/54460dd/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4973x2791+0+0/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F6f%2F4d%2Ff9adf3e14483ae37b446301a8510%2Fpaige-yelling.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/37e6f7a/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4973x2791+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F6f%2F4d%2Ff9adf3e14483ae37b446301a8510%2Fpaige-yelling.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="Semifinals - Unrivaled 2026" srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/37e6f7a/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4973x2791+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F6f%2F4d%2Ff9adf3e14483ae37b446301a8510%2Fpaige-yelling.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/822935f/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4973x2791+0+0/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F6f%2F4d%2Ff9adf3e14483ae37b446301a8510%2Fpaige-yelling.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/37e6f7a/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4973x2791+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F6f%2F4d%2Ff9adf3e14483ae37b446301a8510%2Fpaige-yelling.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>NEW YORK, NEW YORK - MARCH 02: Paige Bueckers #5 of the Breeze reacts to a basket during the first quarter against the Mist in the Unrivaled Semifinals at Barclays Center on March 02, 2026 in New York City. (Photo by Ishika Samant/Getty Images) ORG XMIT: 776461923</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Ishika Samant/Getty</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>It was a compelling sight, watching the next generation of stars growing into themselves.</p><p>Whatever direction Unrivaled takes, it will likely require some level of partnership, or at least coordination, with the WNBA. </p><p>That may seem counterintuitive, since Unrivaled’s identity comes from its independence. Free of the WNBA’s baggage, the league could innovate and compensate players generously.</p><p>But Unrivaled’s founders and executives readily acknowledge that the ecosystem works best when its pieces move together.</p><p>Though a formal partnership may still be a long way off, don’t be shocked if the disruptor eventually becomes part of the machine.</p>
entries.author: Alissa Hirsh

entries.title: Dear Abby: Son confides to mom about grandmother's abusive behavior
entries.summary: <p><b>DEAR ABBY:</b> I am reaching out as a single mother grappling with a serious heart-lung condition. My son's father abandoned us when I was pregnant, and I haven't heard from him in more than a decade. Thankfully, my parents have been supportive co-parents during the years when my health made things incredibly challenging.</p><p>I have always encouraged my son to express his feelings and have assured him that his emotions are valid. We share a strong bond, and he feels comfortable discussing anything with me. Recently, he confided that he feels unsafe at his grandparents' house, where he spends two nights a week. He revealed that his grandmother is verbally abusive and critical — laughing at him when he makes mistakes, calling him a "loser," making sneering comments and speaking poorly of me when they are alone, even though she's pleasant to my face.</p><p>My mother's behavior is deeply troubling. My son is scared to have me confront her because he's worried he will be punished for sharing his experiences. In any other scenario, I would tell my mother that until she chooses to not abuse, he won't be staying over. However, we have a mediated agreement that allows for those two overnights a week. I fear my mother could manipulate the situation and lie to the courts to maintain this arrangement. What should I do? — <i>HOPELESS AND OVERWHELMED IN OREGON</i></p><p><b>DEAR HOPELESS:</b> Something has gone wrong with the arrangement you have with your mother. Any extreme change in behavior is troubling, and if her change of behavior is recent, she may need to be medically evaluated. What you need to do now is discuss this sorry situation with an attorney who may be able to challenge the custody agreement and protect your son from your mother's abuse.</p><p><b>DEAR ABBY:</b> My son "Scot" recently remarried. I wasn't involved in any of the preparations. I was also not acknowledged at the wedding and felt like just another guest. My son decided to change his last name without informing me about it. When I asked why, he said he had no claim to the name even though he has a brother and children with that last name.</p><p>Am I wrong for feeling I've been punched in the heart for not being involved in this decision? The hurt is real. — <i>MOM WHO DOESN'T MATTER</i></p><p><b>DEAR MOM:</b> With this new marriage, Scot is starting over, and the name change may be his way of creating a new beginning. Obviously, you and your son are not close enough that he confides in you, or he might have spoken to you about his decision and explained it beyond feeling he had "no claim" to the name he, his brother and his children were raised with. Scot's decision was a personal one. Whatever his reason, it has nothing to do with you, and it should not be regarded as a "punch in the heart." (A flip of the stomach, perhaps, but in no way related to you.)</p><p><i>Dear Abby is written by Abigail Van Buren, also known as Jeanne Phillips, and was founded by her mother, Pauline Phillips. Contact Dear Abby at www.DearAbby.com or P.O. Box 69440, Los Angeles, CA 90069.</i></p><p>Abby shares more than 100 of her favorite recipes in two booklets: "Abby's Favorite Recipes" and "More Favorite Recipes by Dear Abby." Send your name and mailing address, plus check or money order for $16 (U.S. funds) to: Dear Abby, Cookbooklet Set, P.O. Box 446, Kings Mills, OH 45034-0446. (Shipping and handling are included in the price.)</p>
entries.author: Abigail Van Buren

entries.title: Conservatives, progressives unite to defend weed smoker's Second Amendment rights
entries.summary: <p>Under <a class="Link" href="https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/18/922" target="_blank" >federal law</a>, millions of Americans are committing felonies right now because they own guns and use marijuana, even if they live in states that have legalized the drug. There is nothing unconstitutional about that baffling situation, a Trump administration lawyer <a class="Link" href="https://www.c-span.org/event/public-affairs-event/justices-hear-case-on-gun-ban-for-drug-users/440150" target="_blank" >assured</a> the Supreme Court on Monday, because cannabis consumers are analogous to "habitual drunkards," who historically could be confined to workhouses or mental institutions.</p><p>Most of the justices, including both Republican and Democratic appointees, <a class="Link" href="https://reason.com/2026/03/02/scotus-seems-skeptical-of-the-federal-ban-on-gun-possession-by-cannabis-consumers/" target="_blank" >seemed skeptical</a> of that claim. Their agreement reflected the trans-partisan alliances inspired by this case, which illustrates the potential for <a class="Link" href="https://beyondcontrolbook.net/" target="_blank" >common ground</a> between right-leaning critics of gun control and left-leaning critics of the war on drugs.</p><p>The case, which sits at the intersection of those two policies, involves Ali Hemani, a Texas man who was <a class="Link" href="https://reason.com/2024/06/27/he-faced-a-terrorism-probe-went-to-jail-on-a-gun-charge-and-now-is-charged-with-drug-possession/" target="_blank" >charged</a> with illegal gun possession in 2023 after an FBI search of his home discovered a Glock 19 pistol, about 2 ounces of marijuana, and less than a gram of cocaine. Hemani admitted that the gun was his, and that he smoked marijuana a few times a week, which would have been enough to convict him.</p><div class="RichTextSidebarModule Enhancement" data-module data-align-center><a class="AnchorLink" id="module-be0000" name="module-be0000"></a> <div class="RichTextSidebarModule-title">Columnist </div> <div class="RichTextModule-items RichTextBody">Columnist</div> </div><p>The case never went to trial. The charge was <a class="Link" href="https://www.supremecourt.gov/DocketPDF/24/24-1234/362144/20250602174403309_HemaniPetition.pdf#page=41" target="_blank" >dismissed</a> based on a 2024 <a class="Link" href="https://www.ca5.uscourts.gov/opinions/pub/23/23-50312-CR0.pdf" target="_blank" >decision</a> by the U.S. Court of Appeals for the 5th Circuit, which held that the Second Amendment bars such prosecutions when they are based on nothing beyond the elements specified by the statute.</p><p>Those elements do not require any showing that the defendant's pattern of drug use disrupts his own life, let alone that it poses a threat to public safety. Yet the Trump administration, despite its <a class="Link" href="https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/2025/02/protecting-second-amendment-rights/" target="_blank" >avowed commitment</a> to "protecting Second Amendment rights," <a class="Link" href="https://reason.com/2025/10/20/scotus-will-consider-the-constitutionality-of-the-federal-ban-on-gun-possession-by-illegal-drug-users/" target="_blank" >wants</a> the Supreme Court to reject the 5th Circuit's logic and reinstate the case against Hemani.</p><p>Counterintuitively, a bunch of blue states that have legalized marijuana are <a class="Link" href="https://reason.com/2026/01/14/19-states-that-legalized-marijuana-use-nevertheless-say-it-should-disqualify-people-from-owning-guns/" target="_blank" >siding</a> with the Trump administration, condemning a decision in which the country's most conservative appeals court upheld the constitutional rights of cannabis consumers. The attorneys general of those states evidently decided that protecting legislators' discretion to regulate firearms was more important than defending the proposition that marijuana should be treated like alcohol.</p><p>The briefs urging the Supreme Court to uphold the 5th Circuit's ruling also feature some <a class="Link" href="https://reason.com/2026/02/02/the-nra-and-norml-unite-to-oppose-the-federal-gun-ban-for-marijuana-users/" target="_blank" >strange bedfellows</a>. They include the Drug Policy Alliance, the National Organization for the Reform of Marijuana Laws and the National Association of Criminal Defense Lawyers as well as leading Second Amendment groups such as the National Rifle Association, Gun Owners of America and the Firearms Policy Coalition.</p><p>Even the American Civil Liberties Union, which has not previously shown much interest in defending the Second Amendment, is <a class="Link" href="https://reason.com/2026/02/25/the-aclu-long-leery-of-the-second-amendment-joins-the-nra-in-urging-scotus-to-uphold-pot-users-gun-rights/" target="_blank" >siding</a> with Hemani. That position is pretty striking because the ACLU <a class="Link" href="https://reason.com/2019/04/12/the-aclu-defends-the-rights-of-gun-owner/" target="_blank" >argued</a> for decades that the right Hemani wants to vindicate does not exist.</p><p>"This is the first time that we have entered a case affirmatively on behalf of an individual making a Second Amendment claim," said Brandon Buskey, director of the ACLU's Criminal Law Reform Project and one of the attorneys listed in <a class="Link" href="https://www.supremecourt.gov/DocketPDF/24/24-1234/392632/20260123152824381_24-1234%20Brief%20for%20Respondent.pdf" target="_blank" >Hemani’s brief</a>. "Now that the Supreme Court has recognized this as a fundamental right, we see this as an important civil liberties issue."</p><p>To justify restrictions on that right, the Supreme Court has <a class="Link" href="https://www.supremecourt.gov/opinions/21pdf/20-843_7j80.pdf" target="_blank" >said</a>, the government must show they are "consistent with this Nation's historical tradition of firearm regulation."</p><p> It is not hard to see why the Second Amendment groups think Hemani's prosecution fails that test.</p><p>The Trump administration's argument hinges on equating even occasional or moderate cannabis consumers with people who would have been deemed "habitual drunkards" at the founding. As Justices Neil Gorsuch and Sonia Sotomayor noted during Monday's oral argument, that comparison makes little sense.</p><p>Even people who are inclined to support gun control should be able to see the problems with the government's analogy, which aims to treat people as criminals for no good reason. The resulting injustice is palpable enough that it has provoked outrage from Americans across the political spectrum.</p><p><i>Jacob Sullum is a senior editor at Reason magazine.</i></p><div class="RichTextSidebarModule Enhancement" data-module data-align-center><a class="AnchorLink" id="module-e70000" name="module-e70000"></a> <div class="RichTextModule-items RichTextBody">Send letters to <a class="Link" href="mailto:letters@suntimes.com" target="_blank" >letters@suntimes.com</a>. More about <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/submitting-opinion-and-letters" >how to submit here</a>.</div> </div><p></p>
entries.author: Jacob Sullum

entries.title: Ojala Islamic Center, the first Latino-led mosque in the Midwest, opens in Berwyn
entries.summary: <p>In the basement of one of the Chicago area's newest places of worship, all the fixings of a traditional Mexican meal were laid out to enjoy.</p><p>“We have mole, birria, spaghetti verde, arroz. We even have flautas,” Cesar Cortina, 39, said Sunday evening at the space. </p><p>It’s a spread one might find following services at any of Chicago’s many predominately Mexican Roman Catholic churches.</p><p>But this was not at a church.</p><p>It was at the first mosque in the Midwest geared toward Latino Muslims, and one of only a handful nationwide. Ojala Islamic Center opened Feb. 17, the first day of Ramadan. The center is in a former church in a quiet neighborhood in west suburban Berwyn.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-150000" name="image-150000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/eb34423/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6208x3484+0+328/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F89%2F5e%2F51aadd1e46ddb138082a8982b8d9%2Flatino-muslims-260304-022.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/5092dcc/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6208x3484+0+328/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F89%2F5e%2F51aadd1e46ddb138082a8982b8d9%2Flatino-muslims-260304-022.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/923a5a6/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6208x3484+0+328/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F89%2F5e%2F51aadd1e46ddb138082a8982b8d9%2Flatino-muslims-260304-022.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="Members and guests enjoy an iftar spread at Ojala Islamic Center in Berwyn, Sunday, March 1, 2026. Ojala is the first Latino mosque in the Midwest." srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/923a5a6/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6208x3484+0+328/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F89%2F5e%2F51aadd1e46ddb138082a8982b8d9%2Flatino-muslims-260304-022.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/a747c4c/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6208x3484+0+328/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F89%2F5e%2F51aadd1e46ddb138082a8982b8d9%2Flatino-muslims-260304-022.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/923a5a6/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6208x3484+0+328/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F89%2F5e%2F51aadd1e46ddb138082a8982b8d9%2Flatino-muslims-260304-022.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Members and guests enjoy an iftar spread at Ojala Islamic Center in Berwyn on Sunday.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Candace Dane Chambers/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>“It just feels more and more like home to me,” said Cortina, who became a Muslim a year ago. “I love it.”</p><p>“This is needed now because our community is growing at numbers that we've never seen before,” said Imam Christopher AbdulKareem Pavlicek, 48, the center's director.</p><p>“We knew eight years ago when we started this work that there was a need," Pavlicek said. "As we continue to do work in the community, the community just started growing at such a rapid rate. We didn’t necessarily have places to meet on our own. Having this place, it was a perfect time for us.”</p><p>Latinos make up a small but growing part of the Muslim population in the U.S., with Pew Research <a class="Link" href="https://www.pewresearch.org/politics/2011/08/30/section-1-a-demographic-portrait-of-muslim-americans/" target="_blank" >polls</a> <a class="Link" href="https://www.pewresearch.org/religion/2017/07/26/demographic-portrait-of-muslim-americans" target="_blank" >showing</a> practicing members of the faith rose from 6% to 8% between 2011 and 2017.</p><p>The effort that eventually led to the mosque opening last month started more than a decade ago when there wasn't much for Latino Muslims in the Chicago area and no space of their own.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-fc0000" name="image-fc0000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275"
